creator: bofh@freebsd.org
text:
Seems like alright. Go ahead.


On Sat, Jan 5, 2013 at 3:38 PM, Ruslan Makhmatkhanov <cvs-src@yandex.ru>wrote:

> Muhammad Moinur Rahman wrote on 05.01.2013 12:40:
>
>> The following reply was made to PR ports/174991; it has been noted by
>> GNATS.
>>
>> From: Muhammad Moinur Rahman <5u623l20@gmail.com>
>> To: "bug-followup@FreeBSD.org" <bug-followup@freebsd.org>
>> Cc:
>> Subject: Re: ports/174991: net-mgmt/argus: request for deprecation
>> Date: Sat, 5 Jan 2013 14:38:53 +0600
>>
>
>    Hi,
>>
>>   Agreed with you. And would recommend the following :
>>   1. Depreciate net-mgmt/argus-monitor
>>   2. Repocopy net-mgmt/argus3-clients to net-mgmt/argus-clients and
>>   depreciate net-mgmt/argus3-clients
>>   3. Repocopy net-mgmt/argus3 to net-mgmt/argus and depreciate
>> net-mgmt/argus3
>>
>>   Looking forward for committer's advice.
>>
>>   Regards,
>>   Muhammad
>>
>
> It looks like that argus-monitor is not related. It's an standalone
> monitoring tool that do not relay on argus.. As for repocopies, I think
> it's better to do that after deprecation period and removing of argus2
> ports to let it's potential users time to migrate to argus3. Please let me
> know if it's ok with you.
>
> --
> Regards,
> Ruslan
>
> Tinderboxing kills... the drives.
>
